underlying: Number of transit calls through the Strait of Hormuz, IMF PortWatch, week 4/27-5/3 2026
method: Mixture model. Resolution rule (Kalshi): 'If the number of transit calls through the Strait of Hormuz as reported by the IMF PortWatch is above $X from Apr 27, 2026 to May 3, 2026, then the market resolves Yes.' Settlement source = IMF PortWatch chokepoint6 weekly aggregate. Underlying state: traffic catastrophically depressed since US/Iran blockade Feb 2026; pre-crisis baseline ~138 vessels/day (~966/week). Recent daily counts: 3-8 ships/day late April (8 reported on 4/26 per HormuzStraitMonitor; range 3-7 days prior). Implied 4/27-5/3 weekly point estimate: 6.5/day * 7 = ~42-46 vessels (open of week aligns with current trickle regime). Model: 85% probability of 'baseline trickle regime' ~ N(42, 8) representing day-to-day Poisson noise + minor incident variability; 15% probability of 'partial reopening regime' ~ N(100, 40) representing a real ceasefire/de-escalation midweek that allows ship traffic to recover toward 80-180/week (still well below pre-crisis 966). YES probability above strike s = 0.85*(1-Phi((s-42)/8)) + 0.15*(1-Phi((s-100)/40)), clipped to [0.005, 0.995]. The mixture is necessary because Normal-only undershoots the right-tail that Kalshi market is paying (T80 YES=0.11, T100 YES=0.065, T140 YES=0.05).
notes: Confidence MEDIUM-LOW because: (a) PortWatch counts have known data-quality issues — GPS jamming, AIS spoofing, vessels going dark per Wikipedia 2026 Hormuz Crisis page — so the official weekly figure can deviate non-trivially from raw observed counts; (b) the regime-shift probability (15%) is a judgment call from news flow (Iran proposal to US per recent reports, Apr 17 reopening per prior notes), could be 5-25% reasonable range; (c) this is the first week of the question on this strait under active blockade, so no calibration history. Edge picture vs market: T40 model 0.65 vs market mid 0.62 (essentially flat); T50 model 0.27 vs market 0.37 (sell YES at T50 = small edge, ~10c); T60 model 0.14 vs market 0.20 (sell YES at T60 ~6c edge); upper tail T80-T140 model and market roughly aligned. Most-tradeable view: SELL YES at T50, T60 if size available; otherwise no clear directional bet. Path risk: if a real ceasefire signs midweek, this whole vector goes to ~0.6-0.8 across mid strikes overnight.
